opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach has this bright red, weeping rash that itches so much it's driving me crazy. The creams the pharmacist gave me did nothing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about four days ago and has got much worse — it's now oozing and crusting, the itch is unbearable and I couldn't sleep, and the antiseptic cream I've been putting on hasn't helped at all.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 4 days ago, starting as dry scaly skin on my lower belly and then getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.duration: around 4 days and definitely worse now, not better
symptom_narrative.character: a bright red, sharply-edged patch that itches and burns; from scratching it has started weeping clear fluid and getting a bit of yellowish crust and small cracks in the skin
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is so bad it's driving me crazy and I couldn't sleep; I keep having to lift my shirt to scratch at it
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on my lower belly, just below my belly button
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse over the last few days, worst when I scratch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much; a cool damp cloth eases the itch for a moment
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching makes it weep more, and the antiseptic cream I've been using seems to make it sting and worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever or chills, I don't feel unwell in myself, and it hasn't spread anywhere else — it's just this one patch on my belly
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't think I've had anything quite like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I couldn't sleep because of the itching and I keep getting distracted by it during the day

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: an over-the-counter antiseptic cream (Savlon) that I've been putting on the rash myself for a few days

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I bought a new belt recently and the rash is exactly where its metal buckle sits against my skin — the belt is here on the chair next to me

objective_data_offered.value: a bright red, sharply-edged roughly rectangular patch on my lower belly, about the size of a matchbox, that's weeping clear fluid with a bit of yellowish crust and some scratch marks and tiny cracks
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described and shown by the patient over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: it's a square-ish shape exactly where the metal buckle of my new belt presses on my skin, just below the belly button — nowhere else on my body
uncomfortable and tired, keeps lifting shirt to scratch at the belly
I haven't taken my temperature but I don't feel hot, shivery or unwell in myself
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken

objective_data_offered.value: the new belt with the shiny metal buckle is on the chair beside me
